heroSubtitle: Learn to set up your tattoo machine, and the logic behind it.
heroLede: Master your machine, no matter the brand. Get pro-level voltage, needle, and setup guidance for your style—designed for artists who value precision over hype.
step1Body: Select your style. This establishes the physics required for the skin you are working on.
step2Body: Define the hit (Lining vs Shading) to activate our safety guard against skin trauma.
step3Body: Pick your machine—whether it's a Tier 2 workhorse or a Tier 1 benchmark. The engine adjusts to your specific hardware limits.
step4Body: Use the Hand Speed slider to match your movement to the motor frequency. Watch the CPS gauge to ensure you aren't outrunning your needle.
strokeBaselineHint: Recommended stroke baseline for {style}: {mm} mm — when you pick a machine, stroke snaps to the closest available option.
handSpeedAriaLabel: Hand speed relative to machine cycle rate
handSpeedNote: Note: Hand speed is a relative variable. This scale is designed to demonstrate the conceptual relationship between movement and cycle frequency, not as a literal measurement.
needleHangDisclaimer: While we recommend brand-standard needle hang, setup remains a personal choice dictated by technical skill and artistic intent.
recommendedPathHint: Recommended path is highlighted when both style and machine are set.
gaugePlaceholder: Select style, technique, and machine to load recommended readouts and gauges.
hzCaptionAcus: For ACUS, treat Hz as the main operating readout; voltage is a bench supply reference into that band.
hzCaptionOther: Heuristic band tied to voltage—similar to how many bench supplies frame the drive readout.
cpsCaption: CPS = round((final volts × 1000) ÷ 60)—a scalar teaching view of how hard the pack is being driven after hand-speed offset and clamp.
lessonBody: tattoomachinesetup.com has adapted your voltage for a {currentStroke} mm stroke. For {style}, the industry standard is {idealStroke} mm to ensure optimal saturation without skin trauma.
lessonLink: Learn more about the 3.5mm pivot and stroke physics.
sltContextBody: Brand bridge equivalents (SLT, #10, TX) appear in glossary hovers on matching cartridge copy — main UI stays standard-first.
voltDetailAdapted: Operating band {vmin} – {vmax} V (3.5 mm pivot offset {mod} V).
voltDetailBaseline: Machine baseline {baseline} V → soft-guard / stroke adjusted {adj} V
proTipRealism: Using a 4.2mm stroke for realism requires a very light hand — let needle work do the heavy lifting.
proTipLongStroke: Long stroke (4.0mm+) with Soft Shading: baseline voltage is reduced by 1.5V to limit skin trauma.
proTipHang: Higher hanging with soft techniques: float the needle and watch ink saturation — avoid over-driving voltage.
